prompt: capture today's learnings from the kanban workshop: WIP limits prevent overload by capping in-flight work, and pull-based systems generally beat push-based because they self-regulate to capacity. both fit our team but the WIP limit one is the one I keep coming back to.

assertions.description: Summary or decision-log explicitly states the input contained multiple claims (compound), not a single idea
Either two-or-more separate zettel files are written, or a split plan is documented in decision-log.md before deciding what to write
No single .md file contains BOTH the WIP-limits definition AND the pull-vs-push generalization mashed together
Every emitted zettel file has [[product]] in its H1
Every emitted zettel file ends with `Index: [[product]]` footer
Each emitted card has at least one outbound wikilink in the body — they may cross-link, or link to other existing zettels
Output filenames are meaningful kebab-slugs (e.g. wip-limits.md, pull-vs-push.md), not generic names like card-1.md
Subjective: summary.md acknowledges the atomicity gate and explains the split decision in a way that shows the skill's principles were applied
